channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Simon Willison] Quoting Karen Kwok for Reuters Breakingviews
item.description: Quoting Karen Kwok for Reuters Breakingviews

item.title: [Simon Willison] How we contain Claude across products
item.description: How we contain Claude across products

item.title: [Hacker News] Domain expertise has always been the real moat
item.description: Domain expertise has always been the real moat

item.title: [Hacker News] Hormuz crisis side effect: a sharp rise in container shipping rates
item.description: Hormuz crisis side effect: a sharp rise in container shipping rates

item.title: [Hacker News] OpenRouter raises $113M Series B
item.description: OpenRouter raises $113M Series B

item.title: [Hacker News] AI job grief: A psychological crisis hitting tech workers
item.description: AI job grief: A psychological crisis hitting tech workers

item.title: [Hacker News] Memory decline after menopause linked to loss of estrogen production in brain
item.description: Memory decline after menopause linked to loss of estrogen production in brain

item.title: [Hacker News] Anthropic surpasses OpenAI to become most valuable AI startup
item.description: Anthropic surpasses OpenAI to become most valuable AI startup

item.title: [GitHub Trending] OpenBMB/VoxCPM — VoxCPM2: Tokenizer-Free TTS for Multilingual Speech Generation, Creative Voice Design, and True-to-Life Cloning
item.description: VoxCPM2 is a tokenizer-free text-to-speech system enabling multilingual speech generation, voice design, and cloning.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] FareedKhan-dev/train-llm-from-scratch — A straightforward method for training your LLM, from downloading data to generating text.
item.description: Repository provides step-by-step instructions for training language models from data collection through text generation.

item.title: [Hacker News] Corporate America Is Starting to Ration AI as Cost Skyrockets
item.description: Corporate America Is Starting to Ration AI as Cost Skyrockets

item.title: [Hacker News] Show HN: Helios – what plug-in solar could generate for any address in Britain
item.description: Show HN: Helios – what plug-in solar could generate for any address in Britain

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Founders and Forward Deployed Engineers
item.description: [AINews] Founders and Forward Deployed Engineers

item.title: [Hacker News] Show HN: Tiny-vLLM – high performance LLM inference engine in C++ and CUDA
item.description: Show HN: Tiny-vLLM – high performance LLM inference engine in C++ and CUDA

item.title: [Hacker News] Shift will clean homes for free to train future robots
item.description: Shift will clean homes for free to train future robots

item.title: [Hacker News] Notes from the Mistral AI Now Summit in Paris
item.description: Notes from the Mistral AI Now Summit in Paris

item.title: [Hacker News] Liquid AI reveals 8B-A1B MoE trained on 38T
item.description: Liquid AI reveals 8B-A1B MoE trained on 38T

item.title: [Hacker News] Please Use AI
item.description: Please Use AI

item.title: [Hacker News] Expertise in the age of AI
item.description: Expertise in the age of AI

item.title: [OpenAI] Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses
item.description: Boston Children’s uses AI to unlock new diagnoses

item.title: [OpenAI] How Braintrust turns customer requests into code with Codex
item.description: How Braintrust turns customer requests into code with Codex

item.title: [Hacker News] Is AI causing a repeat of Front end's Lost Decade?
item.description: Is AI causing a repeat of Front end's Lost Decade?

item.title: [GitHub Trending] anthropics/claude-code — Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows - all through natural language commands.
item.description: Claude Code is a terminal-based AI coding assistant that automates tasks, explains code, and handles git workflows via natural language.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] run-llama/liteparse — A fast, helpful, and open-source document parser
item.description: LiteParse is an open-source document parser offering fast parsing capabilities.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] galilai-group/stable-worldmodel — A platform for reproducible world model research and evaluation
item.description: Galilai-group's stable-worldmodel enables reproducible world model research and evaluation.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Crosstalk-Solutions/project-nomad — Project N.O.M.A.D, is a self-contained, offline survival computer packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere.
item.description: Project N.O.M.A.D combines tools, knowledge, and AI into a self-contained offline computer system.

item.title: [Hacker News] Real-time LLM Inference on Standard GPUs: 3k tokens/s per request
item.description: Real-time LLM Inference on Standard GPUs: 3k tokens/s per request

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] One Mask to Rule Them All: On Hidden Facts after Editing and How to Find Them
item.description: Knowledge editing methods ROME and MEMIT rely on a common mechanism identifiable through binary masks that suppress rather than overwrite facts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Representation Signatures and Risk-Feedback Alignment in LLM Trading Agents
item.description: Research identifies pre-failure signatures in LLM trading agents through embedding analysis and shows risk feedback improves alignment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mechanistic origins of catastrophic forgetting: why RL preserves circuits better than SFT?
item.description: Research shows reinforcement learning preserves neural circuits better than supervised fine-tuning during adaptation, explaining superior retention of prior capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Molecular Lead Optimization via Agentic Tool Planning
item.description: TRACE, an LLM-based agent, optimizes drug leads through sequential decision-making while improving ADMET properties and preserving molecular structure.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Self-Play Reinforcement Learning under Imperfect Information in Big 2
item.description: Researchers benchmark self-play RL algorithms on Big 2 card game, finding PPO superior in handling imperfect information and non-stationary opponents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Emergent Semantic Representations in World Models through Physical Interaction without Linguistic Supervision
item.description: World models trained on embodied exploration develop spatial semantic representations mirroring physical geometry without linguistic supervision.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Continuity and Ordinality Matter: Constraining Time Series Tokens for Effective Time Series Analysis with Large Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose COM, a strategy integrating geometric constraints to preserve continuity and ordinality in time series token embeddings for improved LLM performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PrismFlow: Residual Dynamics for Flow Matching in Time-Series Generation
item.description: PrismFlow proposes multiple dynamical experts with residual corrections to improve Flow Matching for multimodal time-series generation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TaxDistill: Improving Metagenomic Taxonomic Annotation via Distilled Genomic Foundation Models
item.description: TaxDistill uses knowledge distillation with a 500M-parameter genomic foundation model to improve metagenomic taxonomic annotation by reducing label noise.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Balancing Multimodal Learning through Label Space Reshaping
item.description: Researchers propose BMLR, a method that reshapes the label space to balance multimodal learning by equalizing mapping difficulty across modalities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Representation Alignment Rests on Linear Structure
item.description: Linear structure in representations explains cross-model alignment through signal, bias, and noise components in modern AI architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Pre-Registering the Detectable Effect: A Paired-MDE Budget for 4-bit Quantization Benchmarks, with a Pilot Audit
item.description: Researchers propose a pre-registration method for quantization benchmarks using minimum detectable effect budgets, finding most reported unreliability is statistical sampling noise.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Continuous-time Causal Foundation Models
item.description: Study extends discrete-time causal models to continuous time using SDEs with observation-schedule invariance, outperforming naive integration.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Context Distillation as Latent Memory Management
item.description: Context distillation framework uses modular LoRA adapters with memory retrieval and self-gating to efficiently manage multiple distilled latent memories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Feature Geometry of LoRA Adapters: A Sparse Autoencoder Analysis of Representational Divergence in Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: LoRA fine-tuning induces representational changes that geometrically diverge from the original pretrained model's feature space.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Spectral Guidance for Flexible and Efficient Control of Diffusion Models
item.description: Spectral Guidance controls diffusion models via label, CLIP, and mask guidance by projecting onto learned singular functions without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Sequential Physics-Constrained Neural Operator Forward Modeling for the $\textit{Norne}$ Reservoir System
item.description: Physics-informed neural operators achieve 99% accuracy in three-phase reservoir forecasting with proven long-term stability bounds.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Cycle-Space Informed Detection of Autoencoded Blind False Data Injection Attacks on Power Systems
item.description: Researchers propose a topology-informed Cycle-Space Detector to detect Autoencoder-based false data injection attacks on power systems using network structural constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When LLM Reward Design Fails: Diagnostic-Driven Refinement for Sparse Structured RL
item.description: Iterative diagnostic-driven refinement of LLM-generated reward functions improves performance on sparse structured RL tasks better than one-shot generation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CosmicFish-HRM: Adaptive Reasoning via Hierarchical Recurrent Mechanisms in Compact Language Models
item.description: CosmicFish-HRM is a compact language model with adaptive reasoning that dynamically allocates computational effort based on input complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Conf-Gen: Conformal Uncertainty Quantification for Generative Models
item.description: Conf-Gen extends conformal risk control methodology to generative models including LLMs and image generators with formal uncertainty guarantees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Training-Time Diagnostic for Generalization via the Log-Alignment Ratio
item.description: Log-alignment ratio quantifies memorization versus generalization during training by measuring parameter-activation alignment without requiring validation data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Comparing Post-Hoc Explainable AI Methods for Interpreting Black-Box EEG Models in Depression Detection
item.description: Five post-hoc explainability methods compared for EEG-based depression detection showed partial convergence with emphasis on frontal and temporal brain regions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Hamilton-Jacobi Theory of Deep Learning
item.description: Neural network training is reformulated as solving Hamilton-Jacobi initial-value problems, providing a unified theoretical framework across architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learning Robust and Task-Invariant Functional Representation from fMRI through Siamese Self-Supervised Learning
item.description: BrainSimSiam, a self-supervised learning framework, learns generalizable brain imaging representations from limited fMRI data, outperforming supervised baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FormInv: A Measurement Protocol for Semantic Invariance in Mathematical Reasoning Benchmarks
item.description: FormInv protocol reveals paraphrase errors in math benchmarks cause ranking shifts invisible to single-model evaluation, with semantic consistency varying 32 points versus accuracy's 10-point span.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FedQHD: Closed-Form Function-Space Federated Reinforcement Learning
item.description: FedQHD proposes federated Q-learning using random-feature encoders to enable closed-form aggregation across heterogeneous client models in decentralized reinforcement learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LoRe: Adaptive Interaction-Evaluation Routing with Per-Step Interaction Budgets for Iterative Graph Solvers
item.description: LoRe, a training-free wrapper, dynamically routes computation to high-conflict interactions, achieving 8× speedup and 12× memory reduction in neural combinatorial solvers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Causal Intelligence for Constraint-Aware Intervention Design to Induce State Transitions
item.description: COAST uses causal inference to design constrained interventions that transition systems between desired states while balancing efficacy, complexity, and stability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Label-Free Reinforcement Learning via Cross-Model Entropy
item.description: Researchers propose Cross-Model Entropy as a label-free reward signal for LLM reinforcement learning, improving open-ended instruction following without human labels.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ChildEval: When large language models meet children's personalities
item.description: ChildEval is a benchmark with 29K child persona profiles that evaluates whether LLMs can infer and follow children's preferences in conversations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TARQ: Tail-Aware Reconstruction Quantization for Rare-Word Robust Automatic Speech Recognition
item.description: Post-training quantization method for speech recognition that improves accuracy on rare words by rebalancing calibration toward infrequent terms without supervision.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Playing with Words, Improving with Rewards: Training Language Models for Creative Association
item.description: LLMs trained on Codenames using reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards show scale-dependent improvements in creativity versus reasoning performance.
